levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module gives the system architecture overview for the Hybrid Electric Architecture Overview applicable to the AMPEL360E Wide Tube-and-Wing Family baseline.

The module identifies the top-level architectural arrangement, functional layers, principal system interfaces, control boundaries, monitoring provisions, and traceability context for ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

levelledPara.title: Architecture Scope
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture includes the aircraft-level arrangement of energy conversion, electrical power distribution, propulsion drive equipment, thermal management, control and monitoring functions, maintenance interfaces, and degraded-mode coordination logic.

This module is architecture-descriptive only. It does not provide removal, installation, servicing, operational, or troubleshooting procedures. Those subjects shall be defined in the applicable procedural, servicing, fault isolation, and test data modules.

levelledPara.title: Architectural Layers
levelledPara.para: The system architecture is organized into the following controlled functional layers:

listItem.para: Thermal management and heat rejection layer.
Control, monitoring, indication, and recording layer.
Maintenance, diagnostics, and ground support interface layer.

levelledPara.title: Energy Source and Conversion Layer
levelledPara.para: The energy source and conversion layer provides the controlled supply of propulsion energy to the hybrid-electric architecture. Depending on the applicable programme configuration, this layer may include energy storage equipment, fuel-cell or other conversion equipment, auxiliary buffering equipment, contactors, isolation devices, and energy conditioning functions.

levelledPara.title: Electrical Distribution and Protection Layer
levelledPara.para: The electrical distribution and protection layer routes electrical power between the energy conversion equipment, propulsion drive equipment, aircraft electrical loads, and applicable auxiliary systems. This layer includes protection, isolation, switching, monitoring, and fault containment functions.

levelledPara.title: Propulsion Drive Layer
levelledPara.para: The propulsion drive layer converts controlled electrical power into propulsive mechanical output. It includes motor drive equipment, electric machines, associated control electronics, local sensors, protection functions, and interfaces to the propulsion control system.

levelledPara.title: Thermal Management Layer
levelledPara.para: The thermal management layer maintains the temperature of hybrid-electric equipment within defined operating limits. It includes heat acquisition, transfer, rejection, monitoring, and control functions associated with energy conversion equipment, electrical distribution equipment, propulsion drive equipment, and applicable aircraft thermal interfaces.

levelledPara.title: Control and Monitoring Layer
levelledPara.para: The control and monitoring layer coordinates system operating modes, supervises limits, records health data, provides crew and maintenance indications, and supports protective responses to abnormal conditions. This layer interfaces with aircraft-level control, indication, recording, central maintenance, and diagnostic systems.

levelledPara.title: Maintenance and Ground Support Layer
levelledPara.para: The maintenance and ground support layer provides access to diagnostic information, configuration data, built-in-test results, fault records, maintenance messages, and controlled ground support interfaces. The detailed maintenance logic shall be defined in the applicable maintenance, fault isolation, and test data modules.

levelledPara.title: Major Interfaces
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture has controlled interfaces with the aircraft electrical power system, propulsion control system, cockpit indication and recording systems, central maintenance system, thermal management system, ground support equipment, and applicable aircraft safety and protection functions.

levelledPara.title: Operating Mode Architecture
levelledPara.para: The architecture supports normal, monitored, limited, degraded, and safe-state operating modes. Mode transition conditions, protective thresholds, flight crew indications, dispatch effects, and maintenance consequences shall be specified in the applicable control, operational, fault isolation, and maintenance data modules.

levelledPara.title: Degraded-Mode Philosophy
levelledPara.para: The degraded-mode architecture shall prevent single equipment faults from causing uncontrolled propagation across propulsion, electrical, thermal, or control boundaries. Fault containment, isolation, monitoring, and maintenance reporting provisions shall be validated against the applicable system safety and certification baseline.

levelledPara.title: Traceability Context
levelledPara.para: This data module belongs to the ATLAS 070-079 Propulsion Eco-Tech and Hybrid-Electric branch. It supports deterministic traceability between architecture definition, system interface definition, S1000D CSDB publication artefacts, verification evidence, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Authoring Status
levelledPara.para: This issue is an unverified scaffold. The content shall be completed, reviewed, and validated against the applicable BREX data module, business rules, applicability model, terminology rules, programme configuration baseline, and safety assessment evidence before CSDB release.
